insights: Fred Luddy's resolution times degraded monotonically from 118.8 hours in January 2023 to 1540.8 hours in November 2023 — a 13-fold increase — while all other agents maintained stable performance (±23% change), indicating a progressive operational disengagement rather than static inefficiency.

Fred Luddy received zero new tickets after November 21, 2023, whereas the other four agents collectively handled 33 tickets through January 2024, confirming cessation of assignment and suggesting potential offboarding, reassignment, or role change.

Fred Luddy’s extreme resolution time gap (450–587% higher than peers) persists across all priorities, categories, and assignment groups, ruling out ticket complexity or workload distribution as explanatory factors and pointing instead to agent-level systemic failure.

Fred Luddy has 0% negative-resolution-time tickets — in stark contrast to 10–14% for all other agents — and sys_updated_on equals closed_at for all agents, confirming his inflated resolution times reflect actual delayed closures, not data entry artifacts or administrative lag.

In January 2023, Fred Luddy was the top-performing agent (118.8h), outperforming all peers (160–285h), proving his current extreme underperformance is time-bound and acquired — not inherent to skill or role — and likely tied to a specific operational inflection point.

The absence of backlog-clearance behavior (0% early-opened/late-closed tickets) and stable resolution time patterns among peers confirm that Fred’s degradation is isolated, non-recoverable within normal workflow, and requires immediate personnel or process intervention rather than workload rebalancing.
summary: The analysis confirms that Fred Luddy’s extreme resolution time gap — up to 587% above peer averages — is not driven by ticket complexity, data quality issues, or backlog handling, but by a sustained, month-over-month degradation (13x increase Jan–Nov 2023) followed by complete cessation of new assignments after November 21, 2023. His initial strong performance in early 2023 further confirms this is an acquired, time-bound failure mode — not a baseline capability issue. All evidence points to disengagement, role transition, or departure rather than operational overload or systemic process flaws. The other four agents demonstrate consistent, stable performance with no similar trends, reinforcing that the issue is agent-specific and urgent. Recommended next focus: verify Fred Luddy’s current employment or assignment status, activate ticket reassignment protocols for inactive agents, and implement automated early-warning triggers for resolution time degradation exceeding 3x monthly growth.
